prompt:
Help frame the research described in the benchmark case as a concrete business-research problem. Preserve the supplied model and identify claims or information that remain unsupported.

Condition instructions:
Use only the archived frame-business-research-problem Skill materialized from Git tag v0.1.0-alpha at the path supplied by the evaluation runner. Use only the serialized case material.

Benchmark case:
```json
{
  "adversarial": false,
  "decision_time_facts": [
    "capacity limits",
    "product margins",
    "estimated choice parameters"
  ],
  "domain": "OM",
  "evidence_needs": [
    {
      "claim": "Choice parameters represent substitution",
      "source_needed": false,
      "status": "model_assumption"
    },
    {
      "claim": "This policy raises realized profit at a named firm",
      "source_needed": true,
      "status": "unsupported_claim"
    }
  ],
  "fidelity_requirements": [
    "The decision precedes realized arrivals",
    "The objective is contribution margin, not social welfare"
  ],
  "hard_failure_traps": [
    "invented_company_fact",
    "future_or_hidden_information",
    "welfare_claim_from_revenue"
  ],
  "id": "om-01",
  "model_specification": {
    "constraints": [
      "shelf capacity",
      "pick-slot capacity"
    ],
    "decision_variables": [
      "binary product inclusion for the shift"
    ],
    "mathematical_objective": "maximize expected contribution margin",
    "mechanism": "The offered set changes purchase probabilities and substitution, while included products consume scarce operating capacity.",
    "model_type": "capacitated assortment optimization",
    "state_variables": [
      "realized customer arrivals",
      "realized product choices"
    ],
    "uncertainty": "arrival volume and product choice"
  },
  "prohibited_inferences": [
    "Faster delivery is caused by assortment reduction"
  ],
  "prompt": "A neighborhood dark store chooses which products to make orderable for the next shift. Shelf and pick-slot capacity are fixed, demand is uncertain, and unavailable products may redirect demand to displayed substitutes. Frame the research problem without naming a real firm or claiming measured customer effects.",
  "tags": [
    "quick-commerce",
    "assortment",
    "capacity",
    "substitution"
  ],
  "title": "Quick-commerce assortment under pick-slot limits"
}
```
